rationale: The citing paper uses Ahluwalia, Unnava, and Burnkrant (2001) in a concrete methodological role: it explicitly states that, following this prior literature, it measures perceived diagnosticity with relevance/importance items for left vs. right digits. Because perceived diagnosticity is the core proposed mechanism across the studies, this is more than a passing background mention. The citation is not used to extend or challenge the spillover theory itself, but to ground a key construct operationalization.
relation_description: Yan et al. rely on Ahluwalia et al. (2001) as methodological precedent for how to operationalize perceived diagnosticity, adapting that measurement logic to numeric-digit judgments. Thus, the cited paper provides supporting measurement foundation for the mechanism tests in the citing paper.
